perturbation.original_text: Where the Receiving Party uses Confidential Information to maintain or develop software for any person, partnership, joint venture, corporation or other form ofenterprise or customer, it shall take all necessary precautions to ensure that suchConfidential Information, particularly source code, is not disclosed to theseparties, unless specific written permission is obtained from “Black and WhiteInc” in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: Where the Receiving Party uses Confidential Information to maintain or develop software for any person, partnership, joint venture, corporation or other form of enterprise or customer, it shall take all necessary precautions to ensure that suchConfidential Information, particularly source code, is not disclosed to theseparties. Specific written permission is not needed from “Black and WhiteInc”.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires specific written permission from "Black and White Inc" before disclosing confidential information to third parties when developing software. The modified version explicitly states that specific written permission is *not* needed. This directly contradicts the initial requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means nonpublic information that a party to this Agreement (“Disclosing Party”) designates as being confidential to the partythat receives such information (“Receiving Party”) or which, under thecircumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential by theReceiving Party. “Confidential Information” includes, without limitation, information in tangible or intangible form relating to and/or including released or unreleased DisclosingParty software or hardware products, the marketing or promotion of anyDisclosing Party product, Disclosing Party’s business policies or practices,Disclosing Party’s product pricing information, the terms and conditions of anyproposed (or actual) license agreement or other agreement concerningDisclosing Party’s products, license negotiations, and information received fromothers that Disclosing Party is obligated to treat as confidential. In particular, the source code and libraries developed and/or maintained by “Black and White Inc” should be regarded as strictly confidential, even if it isfurther modified or improved by the Receiving Party. Except as otherwise indicated in this Agreement, the term “Disclosing Party” also includes all Affiliates of the Disclosing Party and, except as otherwiseindicated, the term “Receiving Party” also includes all Affiliates of theReceiving Party. An “Affiliate” means any person, partnership, joint venture,corporation or other form of enterprise, domestic or foreign, including but notlimited to subsidiaries, that directly or indirectly, control, are controlled by, orare under common control with a party. Non-Disclosure Agreement Page 2 of 6Where the Receiving Party uses Confidential Information to maintain or develop software for any person, partnership, joint venture, corporation or other form ofenterprise or customer, it shall take all necessary precautions to ensure that suchConfidential Information, particularly source code, is not disclosed to theseparties, unless specific written permission is obtained from “Black and WhiteInc” in advance.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'specific written permission is obtained from “Black and White Inc” in advance' before disclosing confidential information. The changed text explicitly removes this requirement by stating 'Specific written permission is not needed from “Black and White Inc”.' This directly contradicts the previous requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the same section as the Contradicted Text. Therefore, the change creates a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a writtenagreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by bothparties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It can be modified orally, or by written agreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the agreement can *only* be modified in writing. The added text now permits oral modifications, creating a direct contradiction. This could lead to disputes over whether verbal agreements are binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. It shall not be modified except by a writtenagreement dated subsequent to the date of this Agreement and signed by bothparties. None of the provisions of this Agreement shall be deemed to have beenwaived by any act or acquiescence on the part of Disclosing Party, the ReceivingParty, their agents, or employees, but only by an instrument in writing signed byan authorized employee of Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party. No waiverof any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any otherprovision(s) or of the same provision on another occasion.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement could only be modified in writing. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral modifications, which directly contradicts the requirement for written modifications. The contradicted text repeats the same clause, so the change introduces a contradiction in the same section, which falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information orany other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees andconsultants, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way tohelp Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information andprevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party at their convenience, but no later than 30 days, upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information orany other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees andconsultants, and cooperation with Disclosing Party will occur only if Receiving party deems it necessary to regain possession of the Confidential Information andprevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates *immediate* notification upon discovering a breach and *full* cooperation. The change allows for delayed notification (up to 30 days) and cooperation only if Receiving party deems it is needed. This significantly weakens the original obligation and contradicts the requirement for immediacy and full cooperation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (d) Receiving Party shall notify the undersigned Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information orany other breach of this Agreement by Receiving Party and its employees andconsultants, and will cooperate with Disclosing Party in every reasonable way tohelp Disclosing Party regain possession of the Confidential Information andprevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and the contradicted text both state that the Receiving Party must notify the Disclosing Party *immediately* upon discovery of a breach and cooperate in *every reasonable way*. The changed text modifies this to allow notification 'at their convenience, but no later than 30 days' and limits cooperation to only when the Receiving Party deems it necessary. This directly contradicts the 'immediately' and 'every reasonable way' requirements in the contradicted section, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party’s respective successors and lawful assigns; provided, however, that neither partymay assign this Agreement (whether by operation of law, sale of securities orassets, merger or otherwise), in whole or in part, without the prior writtenapproval of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party’s respective successors and lawful assigns; provided, however, that either partymay assign this Agreement (whether by operation of law, sale of securities orassets, merger or otherwise), in whole or in part, without the need for prior written approval of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text forbids assigning the agreement without prior written approval of the other party. The modified text explicitly allows assignment *without* prior written approval, creating a direct contradiction that can undermine the original intent of maintaining control over who the agreement binds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (g) This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of each party’s respective successors and lawful assigns; provided, however, that neither partymay assign this Agreement (whether by operation of law, sale of securities orassets, merger or otherwise), in whole or in part, without the prior writtenapproval of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by explicitly allowing assignment without prior written approval. The contradicted text explicitly prohibits assignment without prior written approval. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the assignment of the agreement, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, thus a strong in-text contradiction. The explanation provided supports this classification as a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction, as the clause is essentially placed in two locations (original and changed) with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party. By disclosing Confidential Information to Receiving Party, DisclosingParty does not grant any express or implied right to Receiving Party to or underany patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secret information except asotherwise provided herein. Disclosing Party reserves without prejudice theability to protect its rights under any such patents, copyrights, trademarks, ortrade secrets except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party. By disclosing Confidential Information to Receiving Party, DisclosingParty grants full ownership to Receiving Party to any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secret information. Disclosing Party gives up the ability to protect its rights under any such patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Disclosing Party retains all rights to the confidential information and does *not* grant any ownership rights to the Receiving Party. The changed text does the opposite, granting *full ownership* to the Receiving Party and relinquishing the Disclosing Party's rights. This creates a fundamental contradiction about who owns the confidential information and intellectual property.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of Disclosing Party. By disclosing Confidential Information to Receiving Party, DisclosingParty does not grant any express or implied right to Receiving Party to or underany patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secret information except asotherwise provided herein. Disclosing Party reserves without prejudice theability to protect its rights under any such patents, copyrights, trademarks, ortrade secrets except as otherwise provided herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is the same as the original text). The original and contradicted text state that the Disclosing Party retains all rights to the confidential information. The changed text grants full ownership to the Receiving Party. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding who owns the confidential information, making it a strong in-text contradiction.
